contract_note: Keep review-gated happy-path evaluation separate from legacy-layout migration coverage. Legacy-layout vaults must route to kb-init before normal compile/query/health operation.

evals.prompt: I have a fresh Obsidian vault at evals/fixtures/needs-setup and want the full review-gated setup for AI engineering notes. Create the required support layer, keep repo/entity expansion optional, and explain what should run next.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-init, creates the required support layer, preserves raw immutability, and keeps downstream output directories optional unless the user asks for them.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-init rather than compile, review, query, or health.
The setup includes raw, wiki, outputs/reviews, AGENTS.md, and CLAUDE.md.
The response preserves the idea that raw notes are immutable.
The response treats repo/entity support and broader outputs scaffolding as optional.

evals.prompt: Inspect evals/fixtures/needs-repair. It has some KB folders already, but the setup is incomplete and I want the workflow repaired before I start compiling new notes. What should run first?
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill to kb-init in repair mode because the support layer is incomplete.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses a repair case rather than compile, review, query, or health.
The recommendation prefers kb-init because the support layer is incomplete.
The answer calls out missing contract files or directories as the decisive signal.

evals.prompt: Inspect evals/fixtures/missing-contract-guidance. The vault already has raw/wiki/outputs structure, but somebody deleted the local AGENTS.md contract file. What should run first before I let anyone compile or query it again?
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill to kb-init in repair mode because AGENTS.md is required even when the rest of the vault is present.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses a repair case rather than compile, review, query, or health.
The recommendation explicitly cites missing AGENTS.md as the decisive signal.
The response does not treat missing only CLAUDE.md as equivalent to missing AGENTS.md.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/needs-migration. This vault still uses direct wiki/summaries and wiki/concepts. Tell me what should run first before I try normal compile/query work.
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill to kb-init because legacy-layout vaults must migrate before normal operation.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-init.
The response identifies the vault as legacy-layout.
The answer says migration is required before normal compile/query/health passes.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/needs-ingest. The vault has a review-gated support layer, but raw/_manifest.yaml is stale relative to raw sources. Tell me which step should run before compile and why.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-ingest because the source registry is stale and should be refreshed before draft compilation continues.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-ingest.
The response treats raw/_manifest.yaml as the canonical source registry.
The answer keeps raw files immutable while recommending a manifest refresh.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/needs-compilation as the target vault. I added a new raw capture under raw/human/articles and want the draft layer updated without touching raw/ or promoting anything to live.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, detects a new raw source, updates only wiki/drafts and the ingest log, and points to kb-review as the next step.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-compile.
The compile pass reports a new raw source.
The response preserves raw immutability.
The response identifies kb-review as the next step.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/bootstrap-raw-intake. The vault already has wiki/index.md and wiki/log.md, but I dropped two markdown notes directly under raw/ and never created a CLAUDE.md. Compile the new sources without rewriting raw files.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, accepts root-level raw notes, treats missing CLAUDE.md as non-blocking, and surfaces the warning without failing.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses this as a compile case rather than an init case.
The compile pass accepts markdown files directly under raw/ as valid sources.
The workflow does not treat missing CLAUDE.md as blocking.
The response preserves raw immutability and surfaces the missing optional file in the summary.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/needs-review. The compile step already produced draft summaries and I want the review gate run now. Approve what is ready, reject what is unsafe, and tell me what should persist.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review, reads pending drafts plus their cited raw captures, and applies the mixed gate between approve, reject, and human review outcomes.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-review.
The review pass stays on drafts, overlapping live pages, and cited raw captures.
The response describes approve/reject/human-review behavior rather than treating the gate as a blind promote step.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/needs-briefing-refresh. The live layer changed after the last briefing build. Rebuild the stale briefing and explain why this is review work rather than query work.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review because needs-briefing-refresh still belongs to the review gate and briefing rebuild lane.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-review.
The response identifies the state as needs-briefing-refresh.
The answer treats briefing rebuild as part of the review boundary, not as normal query behavior.

evals.prompt: Using evals/fixtures/ready-for-query, what do my approved notes say about review gates? Save the answer if it is substantive, and log the query if you archive it.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-query in research mode, checks existing outputs/qa first, and stays grounded in approved live pages and briefings.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-query.
The query pass checks outputs/qa before re-deriving the answer.
The answer stays grounded in wiki/live and briefings rather than drafts or raw.

evals.prompt: Take evals/fixtures/ready-for-query and turn the current approved knowledge into a short X thread about why review gates matter in Obsidian knowledge workflows.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-query in publish mode and saves a grounded publish artifact derived from the approved live layer.
expectations: The workflow uses publish mode rather than only research mode.
The output stays grounded in live pages or prior Q&A.
The workflow identifies where the publish artifact should be saved.

evals.prompt: Run a health pass on evals/fixtures/needs-maintenance. I care about stale Q&A, provenance, and whether the approved layer has started drifting away from the archived answers.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review maintenance mode because the approved live layer has integrity drift that is more urgent than another query.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-review.
The report calls out stale Q&A relative to newer live knowledge.
The response stays on maintenance, provenance, and safe mechanical fixes rather than draft promotion.

evals.prompt: Inspect evals/fixtures/needs-repair-live-only. Somebody left a live summary in place but the rest of the support layer is missing. Which step should run first and why?
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill to kb-init because missing support directories still block normal query work even if some live content exists.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses a repair case rather than kb-query.
The response cites missing wiki/drafts, wiki/briefings, or outputs/reviews as the decisive signal.
The answer does not let existing live content override structural repair requirements.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/paper-intake-with-handle. This vault only has raw/papers plus a sidecar, and it still looks legacy. Tell me what should run first before I try the normal compile loop, and explain what paper handling policy should apply after migration.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-init because this is still a legacy-layout style vault. The response should preserve the rule that raw/papers PDFs go through paper-workbench after migration and treat the sidecar as metadata only.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-init before normal compile work.
The answer treats raw/papers as a paper-routing signal rather than relying on the sidecar to justify routing.
The response preserves the rule that paper-workbench is the required downstream handler.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/paper-intake-missing-handle. This legacy paper vault has a PDF under raw/papers/ but no deterministic paper handle. Tell me the first workflow step and explain how the paper should still be handled after migration.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-init because the vault still needs migration first. After migration, raw/papers should still route through paper-workbench even without a stable handle.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-init before normal compile work.
The response does not fall back to generic pdf handling just because a paper handle is missing.
The answer preserves the directory-policy rule for raw/papers PDFs.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/memory-boundary. The vault has a MEMORY.md for collaboration context. Tell me what should remain queryable truth and what should stay outside the topic retrieval boundary.
evals.expected_output: Explains that approved live pages remain queryable truth while MEMORY.md stays outside the default topic retrieval boundary as collaboration memory.
expectations: The response identifies MEMORY.md as collaboration context rather than approved topic knowledge.
The answer keeps wiki/live as the default retrieval truth layer.
The workflow does not suggest querying MEMORY.md for domain answers by default.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/writeback-backlog. An archived answer already has pending writeback candidates. Tell me which lifecycle step should run first and why this is maintenance rather than a normal query.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review maintenance mode because pending writeback candidates represent maintenance backlog that should be surfaced before normal querying continues.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-review.
The answer identifies pending writeback candidates as the decisive maintenance signal.
The response keeps writeback work inside the draft -> review -> live boundary.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/ready-for-query and explain how optional governance indices like QUESTIONS, GAPS, and ALIASES should be generated from the approved layer without bypassing the review gate.
evals.expected_output: Explains that governance indices can be generated from approved live pages and archived outputs, while durable knowledge still flows through draft -> review -> live.
expectations: The response treats governance indices as optional derived views rather than truth pages.
The answer keeps approved live pages as the default evidence layer.
The workflow does not suggest bypassing review when new durable knowledge is discovered.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/needs-governance-refresh and explain why governance indices should be refreshed from current approved knowledge rather than treated as permanent truth pages.
evals.expected_output: Explains that governance indices are derived maintenance views that should be regenerated from approved live pages and archived outputs.
expectations: The response treats QUESTIONS, GAPS, and ALIASES as derived views.
The answer preserves the review-gated truth boundary.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/governance-enabled-bootstrap and explain when kb-init should generate optional governance scaffolding after the support layer exists.
evals.expected_output: Explains that kb-init may create governance scaffolding after the canonical support layer is established, without making those files part of the truth layer.
expectations: The response treats governance scaffolding as optional.
The answer does not confuse governance indices with approved live notes.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/confidence-decay. Explain why this vault is a maintenance case, which current confidence fields are incomplete, and why overdue review windows should not be treated as structural repair.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review maintenance mode, calls out missing confidence metadata plus an overdue review window, and keeps this as a maintenance problem rather than a setup failure.
expectations: The response frames this as a latest lifecycle maintenance issue.
The answer mentions missing confidence metadata and an overdue review window.
The workflow does not route to kb-init.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/supersession-chain. Explain why the next step is maintenance and what is broken in the supersession chain between the old and new review gate pages.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review maintenance mode and calls out the broken supersession bookkeeping rather than silently choosing one page as truth.
expectations: The response identifies supersession as the key maintenance concern.
The answer does not silently overwrite the older page.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/episodic-consolidation. Explain why the archived Q&A should crystallize into episodic memory and how that still stays outside approved truth until review.
evals.expected_output: Explains the episodic layer, keeps it outside approved truth, and points durable follow-up back into draft/review.
expectations: The response treats outputs/episodes as episodic memory rather than approved truth.
The answer preserves draft -> review -> live for durable knowledge.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/procedural-promotion. Explain why this should become a procedure rather than only another concept page, and what maintenance or review signal is currently missing.
evals.expected_output: Explains the procedural promotion gap and points to wiki/drafts/procedures or wiki/live/procedures as the right landing zone.
expectations: The response names procedural memory explicitly.
The answer does not flatten workflow knowledge back into generic semantic notes.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/graph-relationship-gap. Explain how governance indices and graph export should surface the weak relationship structure without widening the truth boundary.
evals.expected_output: Explains that entities and relationships can be derived into indices and graph JSON, while approved live pages remain the truth surface.
expectations: The response treats the graph as an augmentation layer.
The answer does not let graph export replace approved markdown pages.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/private-shared-boundary. Explain what visibility_scope is for and why private/shared scoping should not be confused with the semantic truth boundary.
evals.expected_output: Explains that visibility_scope is a governance/access signal, not a replacement for the live-vs-episode truth boundary.
expectations: The response distinguishes privacy scope from truth scope.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/hybrid-candidate-routing. Explain which files are approved truth, which ones are only candidate surfaces, and why hybrid retrieval should fuse them without citing them as primary truth.
evals.expected_output: Explains that live pages remain truth while episodes and graph snapshots are candidate-only surfaces for local hybrid retrieval.
expectations: The response keeps candidate surfaces outside the approved truth boundary.
The answer distinguishes MEMORY.md from both truth and candidate retrieval surfaces.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/audit-trail. Explain why a missing operations.jsonl is a maintenance issue once audit scaffolding exists, and what kind of automation should write to it.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review maintenance mode, flags the audit gap, and explains that automation should append machine-readable events instead of silently mutating derived surfaces.
expectations: The response treats missing audit output as maintenance debt.
The answer mentions machine-readable automation events.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/creator-consistency. Explain why this vault is now a creator-consistency maintenance case, including the style-guide/briefing drift, missing reuse bookkeeping in archived content, and the approved summary that is staying underused.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-review maintenance mode and calls out creator consistency drift, reuse gaps, and underused approved sources without widening the truth boundary.
expectations: The response treats style-guide drift as maintenance rather than normal query work.
The answer explicitly mentions creator consistency, reuse bookkeeping, and underused approved sources.
The workflow keeps approved live pages as truth even while auditing creator-facing surfaces.
